mission.title: cert-manager: bugfix: wrong certificate chain is used if preferredChain is configured
mission.description:
### Pull Request Motivation

Due to [recent change of Let's Encrypt Chain of Trust](https://letsencrypt.org/2023/07/10/cross-sign-expiration), the short certificate chain (having ISRG Root X1 as root certificate) became the default certificate provided for `/acme/certificate` API endpoint. Old long certificate chain, which is cross-signed with DST Root CA X3, is now served as an alternate certificate chain.

Caused by this change, if `preferredChain` is configured as `ISRG Root X1` in Issuer or ClusterIssuer, cert-manager now returns a long-chain certificate, cross-signed by DST Root CA, since the current implementation does not include default certificate chain while looking for preferred chain.

Going worse, this does not fall back to the default chain but returns the cross-signed chain since that chain also includes `ISRG Root CA X1` as intermediate CA. 

This PR addresses the issue by 

1. Including default certificate chain while evaluating preferred certificate bundle
2. Checking

steps: Review the issue discussion for context
Apply the fix from the linked pull request
